extra.reasoning: The output acuity depends only on rules r1 (IF sweating THEN emergent) and r2 (IF dyspnea THEN emergent), with default routine. The input variable `fever` appears in NO rule, no priority/tie-breaking mechanism, and the default is fever-independent. Therefore acuity is a function solely of (sweating, dyspnea): emergent if sweating OR dyspnea, else routine. For any fixed (sweating, dyspnea), toggling fever from false to true leaves acuity completely unchanged (acuity_after == acuity_before in all 4 sweating/dyspnea combinations). Since the value is identical, it is trivially "no lower than before," so the monotonicity property (setting fever true never lowers acuity) holds across the entire input space. No counterexample exists.
